title: Golden Brown Scottish Onion Ploughmans Lunch
description: A nutty and chewy Scottish dish featuring caramelized onions and a broiled cheese topping, perfect for a low-carb friendly ploughmans lunch.
instructions: Preheat the broiler to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large skillet, melt the butter over medium heat and add the sliced onions.
Cook the onions for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally, until they are golden brown and caramelized.
In a small bowl, mix together the scotch broth mix, salt, and black pepper.
Sprinkle the scotch broth mix over the caramelized onions and stir to combine.
Transfer the onion mixture to a baking dish and top with the shredded cheddar cheese.
Broil the cheese topping for 2-3 minutes, or until it is melted and golden brown.
Remove the dish from the oven and sprinkle with chopped fresh parsley.
Serve the Golden Brown Scottish Onion Ploughmans Lunch hot, garnished with additional parsley if desired.
